title: The Rainbow.
content:
Look how the rainbow doth appear
    But in one only hemisphere;
    So likewise after our decease
    No more is seen the arch of peace.
    That cov'nant's here, the under-bow,
    That nothing shoots but war and woe.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "The Rainbow.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
